examples.title: PIE for Union of 2 Sets
examples.description: Algorithm to compute cardinality of union of 2 sets
examples.code:
FUNCTION Union2Sets(A, B):
 // A and B are arrays/collections
 countA = SIZE(A)
 countB = SIZE(B)
 countIntersection = SIZE(A ∩ B)
 RETURN countA + countB - countIntersection
examples.timeComplexity: O(min(|A|,|B|)) for intersection + O(1) for formula

examples.title: PIE for 3 Sets
examples.description: Algorithm to compute cardinality of union of 3 sets
examples.explanation: Follows PIE formula: add singles, subtract pairs, add triple

examples.title: General PIE Algorithm
examples.description: General algorithm for n sets using combinatorial summation
examples.code:
FUNCTION PIE_union(sets[1..n]):
 result = 0
 
 FOR k FROM 1 TO n:
 // Get all C(n,k) k-wise intersections
 intersections = allKIntersections(sets, k)
 sumK = SUM(intersections)
 
 IF k IS ODD:
 result = result + sumK // Add
 ELSE:
 result = result - sumK // Subtract
 
 RETURN result

FUNCTION allKIntersections(sets, k):
 // Returns sum of sizes of all C(n,k) k-wise intersections
 // Uses recursion or combinatorial iteration
examples.explanation: General PIE formula: sum over all k-level intersections with alternating signs

examples.title: Derangement Count using PIE
examples.code:
FUNCTION Derangement(n):
 // Method 1: Direct formula
 sum = 0
 FOR k FROM 0 TO n:
 term = (-1)^k / FACTORIAL(k)
 sum = sum + term
 RETURN FACTORIAL(n) × sum

// Alternative: Recursive with memoization
FUNCTION DerangeDP(n, memo):
 IF n == 0: RETURN 1
 IF n == 1: RETURN 0
 IF n IN memo: RETURN memo[n]
 
 memo[n] = (n-1) × (DerangeDP(n-1) + DerangeDP(n-2))
 RETURN memo[n]
examples.explanation: Dₙ = n! × Σ(-1)ᵏ/k! from k=0 to n, or use recurrence Dₙ = (n-1)(Dₙ₋₁ + Dₙ₋₂)
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for DP, O(n) for formula with factorial

examples.title: Counting Multiples using PIE
examples.description: Count integers ≤ N divisible by at least one of given numbers
examples.code:
FUNCTION CountMultiples(N, numbers[1..m]):
 result = 0
 
 // Generate all non-empty subsets of numbers
 FOR subset FROM 1 TO (2^m - 1):
 // Find LCM of numbers in this subset
 lcmVal = LCM_of_subset(numbers, subset)
 count = N / lcmVal // floor division
 
 // Determine sign based on subset size
 k = popcount(subset) // number of elements in subset
 IF k IS ODD:
 result = result + count
 ELSE:
 result = result - count
 
 RETURN result

FUNCTION LCM_of_subset(numbers, subsetMask):
 // Compute LCM only for numbers where bit is set
examples.explanation: Uses PIE: add counts for each divisor, subtract for pairwise LCMs, add for triple LCMs, etc.
